description: Tracks subtle warning signs before a personal crash: sleep notes, missed meals, skipped medication logs, calendar overload, stressful messages, and project churn. It does not diagnose. It simply catches “you are entering the danger zone” earlier.
howToBuild: Private daily check-ins → calendar/task load analysis → optional Gmail metadata only → risk scoring → SMS nudges → private trend page.
monetization: Personal use. Could be adapted as a self-management template.

name: Project Archaeologist
description: Digs through an old project and reconstructs what happened: why files exist, which ideas were abandoned, where the architecture changed, what still works, and what should be deleted before anyone builds on it.
howToBuild: Git log parser → README/notes scan → dependency audit → code map → generated archaeology report → cleanup checklist.
monetization: Dev tool, repo audit service, or paid cleanup package.

name: Public Comment Companion

howToBuild: PDF/URL ingestion → summary and issue extraction → guided questionnaire → draft generator → export/email instructions → source archive.
monetization: Public-interest tool, nonprofit service, or civic education project.

name: Skill Gap Simulator
description: Turns a real goal into a practice world. Instead of saying “learn TypeScript,” it builds tiny drills based on the exact project you want to ship and adapts as you fail or improve.
howToBuild: Goal intake → repo/context scan → drill generator → answer checker → progress log → weekly curriculum update.
monetization: Personalized learning product or coaching companion.

name: Receipts-to-Story Engine
description: Turns mundane artifacts — receipts, photos, screenshots, tickets, notes — into writing prompts and essay seeds. It finds the story hiding in the paper trail.

monetization: Writer tool, memoir workshop asset, or paid creative archive service.

name: Weird Little Business Finder

monetization: Personal productivity, executive assistant template, or coaching tool.

name: Personal Search Engine for “Where Did I Put That?”

howToBuild: Workspace indexing → Gmail/search connectors → markdown snapshots → semantic tags → private search page → answer-with-sources output.

name: Alternative Future Planner

monetization: Personal planning tool, coaching template, or founder decision aid.

name: The “Before You Buy” Bot

name: Small Claims Prep Desk

howToBuild: Intake form → evidence upload → timeline builder → damages calculator → filing checklist → export packet.
monetization: Legal-aid support workflow or personal case organization tool.

name: The Anti-Guru Business Coach

howToBuild: Idea intake → critique rubric → market research → pricing test → landing page brief → decision log.

name: Browser Session Field Notes
description: Turns chaotic web research into field notes. It records what you looked at, what changed your mind, what sources were weak, and what questions remain.
howToBuild: Browser page saving → session notes → source scoring → claim extraction → research memo generator.
monetization: Research workflow for writers, analysts, students, or journalists.

name: Maintenance Oracle for Old Stuff

name: Local Rumor Verifier

name: Personal Dataset Maker

howToBuild: File inventory → extraction scripts → DuckDB ingest → schema.yaml generation → README docs → query examples.
monetization: Data cleanup service, research workflow, or internal tool.

name: Ghost Town Detector for Websites
description: Finds abandoned pages, dead CTAs, stale pricing, outdated docs, broken links, and claims that no longer match the product. It is website maintenance with teeth.
howToBuild: Crawler → link checker → screenshot diff → copy freshness scan → issue dashboard → fix briefs.
monetization: Freelancer audit product, agency maintenance package, or SaaS hygiene tool.

name: Personal Values Diff

howToBuild: Values intake → calendar/task/spending/project review → mismatch detection → private report → monthly check-in.
monetization: Personal development tool or coaching workflow.

name: Source-to-Slide Forge
description: Turns a folder of sources into a sharp slide deck: claims, evidence, visuals, narrative arc, speaker notes, and source list. Useful for talks, pitches, public explainers, or internal briefings.
howToBuild: Source folder ingestion → claim extraction → outline → slide markdown → visual prompts → export to HTML/PDF.
monetization: Research presentation service, creator tool, or internal reporting workflow.

name: The Forgotten Follow-Up Machine
description: Finds people and threads you meant to follow up on but didn’t: old collaborators, kind messages, client leads, introductions, project promises, and “let’s talk soon” ghosts.
howToBuild: Gmail/calendar/messages scan → follow-up detection → priority scoring → draft queue → weekly reminder.
monetization: Freelancer CRM, personal assistant workflow, or relationship maintenance tool.

name: Workshop-in-a-Box Generator
description: Turns any practical topic into a complete workshop: audience, outcomes, exercises, slides, handouts, timing, facilitation notes, and follow-up tasks.

howToBuild: Public zo.space page → source list → manual or scheduled verification checks → status cards → SMS/email digest option → admin approval queue.

name: Secondhand Value Scout

howToBuild: Photo/text intake → web research across resale sites → sold-price table → listing draft generator → margin calculator → saved item database.
monetization: Reseller tool, paid listing prep service, or niche product research.

name: Meeting Shadow Notes
description: Turns rough meeting notes into an accountability layer: decisions, promises, owners, open questions, risks, and “things people carefully avoided saying.” It is less meeting minutes, more memory with teeth.
howToBuild: Notes/transcript upload → extraction prompt → action item database → follow-up email drafts → calendar reminders → private project timeline.
monetization: Team ops tool, freelancer client-management workflow, or personal executive assistant.

name: Policy Change Watchtower
description: Watches specific agency pages, city council agendas, school board packets, or company policy pages and alerts when language changes. It stores before/after diffs so changes cannot quietly vanish.
howToBuild: URL watchlist → scheduled page fetch → text extraction and diffing → DuckDB change log → email/SMS alerts → public archive route if approved.

name: The Kind Resignation Builder
description: Helps someone leave a role without lighting unnecessary fires. It builds a resignation plan, transition checklist, asset handoff, message drafts, and a boundary script for the weird conversations.
howToBuild: Situation intake → timeline builder → task checklist → document handoff folder → email drafts → calendar reminders for transition steps.
monetization: Career coaching asset, HR template, or personal tool.

name: Repair or Replace Judge
description: Decides whether to repair, replace, sell, or live with a broken item. It pulls purchase date, warranty, local repair options, parts cost, used replacement cost, and environmental notes into one decision brief.
howToBuild: Item intake → receipt/warranty lookup → local maps/web research → parts/replacement price search → decision rubric → saved repair log.
monetization: Consumer tool, repair-shop lead magnet, or household ops template.

name: Creator Back Catalog Miner
description: Finds reusable assets hiding in old posts, notes, videos, newsletters, and drafts: ideas that can become guides, paid templates, short posts, lead magnets, or product docs.
howToBuild: Folder/Substack/RSS import → topic clustering → evergreen score → asset recommendation cards → draft generator → approval queue.
monetization: Creator revenue workflow, content audit service, or Substack companion.

name: Family Tech Translator
description: Explains confusing tech tasks for non-technical relatives in plain steps: setting up 2FA, checking scams, exporting photos, canceling a subscription, reading a medical portal message, or backing up files.
howToBuild: Task intake → safe instruction generator → screenshot-friendly checklist → optional SMS/email send → saved family playbook with repeated tasks.
monetization: Personal use, senior tech support service, or local workshop material.

name: Silent Churn Detector
description: Watches for signs that a customer, sponsor, reader, or client is fading before they fully disappear: slower replies, fewer opens, fewer visits, unpaid invoices, reduced usage, or weaker signals.
howToBuild: CRM/customer CSV import → Gmail/newsletter/payment signal checks → risk scoring → follow-up suggestions → weekly retention brief.
monetization: Small business retention tool or consultant service.

name: The Donation Page Doctor
description: Audits a nonprofit, mutual aid, or community donation page for trust, clarity, urgency, proof, payment friction, and follow-up. Then it drafts a cleaner page and thank-you flow.
howToBuild: URL intake → page analysis → trust checklist → copy rewrite → Stripe/payment flow notes → email thank-you sequence draft.
monetization: Nonprofit consulting product, local civic service, or free public-good tool.

name: “Where Did This Come From?” File Detective
description: Looks at a mysterious file and reconstructs likely origin, purpose, creation context, related files, and whether it can be safely deleted or archived.
howToBuild: File picker → metadata inspection → nearby folder scan → content summary → duplicate search → decision report with keep/archive/delete recommendation.
monetization: Personal file cleanup tool or digital decluttering service.

name: Community Skill Exchange Board
description: Creates a local board where people trade practical help: rides, tutoring, yard work, computer help, cooking, repair, childcare swaps, and tool lending. It includes moderation and clear boundaries instead of chaos.
howToBuild: Public zo.space board → submission form → private moderation queue → tag filters → email/SMS notifications → simple trust/safety rules page.
monetization: Community project, sponsor-supported board, or local membership product.

name: Software Migration Sherpa
description: Helps move from one tool to another without losing the plot: Notion to markdown, Google Drive to local folders, Substack to archive, Airtable to DuckDB, old site to new site.
howToBuild: Source/destination intake → export checklist → file converter scripts → validation report → migration log → rollback notes.
monetization: Migration service, paid template, or technical consulting workflow.

name: The “Should I Say Yes?” Filter
description: Turns vague opportunities into a decision brief: actual cost, time load, upside, values fit, deadline pressure, hidden obligations, and what saying yes blocks.
howToBuild: Opportunity intake form → calendar/task load check → rubric scoring → counterfactual notes → decision archive → follow-up reminder.
monetization: Coaching tool, freelancer boundary system, or personal operating workflow.

name: Local Disaster Prep Binder
description: Builds a practical emergency binder for a specific place: evacuation routes, shelters, alerts, medication lists, pet plans, documents, local hazards, phone numbers, and offline printables.
howToBuild: Location intake → local emergency source research → checklist generator → private document vault → printable PDF/markdown binder → seasonal review reminders.
monetization: Public safety project, local workshop product, or family preparedness service.

name: Contract Red Flag Reader
description: Reads a contract, proposal, lease, or service agreement and explains the weird parts: auto-renewal, liability, payment timing, IP ownership, termination, vague deliverables, and missing protections. Not legal advice. Better questions.
howToBuild: PDF/doc upload → clause extraction → red-flag checklist → plain-English summary → question list → negotiation email draft.
monetization: Freelance admin tool, consultant service, or small business intake product.

name: The Local Price Memory
description: Tracks the prices of local basics over time: motel rooms, gas, groceries, firewood, storage units, event tickets, repair services. Useful when “that seems high” needs proof.
howToBuild: Manual capture form + scheduled web checks → price table in DuckDB → charts on private/public page → change alerts → monthly summary.
monetization: Local data newsletter, household budget tool, or community research project.

name: Tiny Apprenticeship Builder
description: Turns someone’s real goal into a tiny apprenticeship: observe examples, copy one, modify one, build one, explain one. It uses their actual files and projects instead of fake exercises.
howToBuild: Goal intake → example finder → exercise generator → file-based practice tasks → answer review → progress log.
monetization: Personalized learning product, tutoring add-on, or internal training tool.

name: Source-of-Truth Cleaner
description: Finds where reality is split across too many files: three pricing docs, two bios, old policies, duplicate specs, conflicting notes. It builds a merge plan and marks stale copies.
howToBuild: Folder scan → similarity/duplicate detection → conflict extraction → source-of-truth recommendation → update checklist → archive script after approval.
monetization: Ops cleanup service, documentation audit, or personal knowledge-base tool.

name: Good Enough Launch Judge
description: Looks at a project and decides whether it is launchable, embarrassing-but-useful, or still fake. It checks broken links, missing docs, core flows, copy clarity, performance, setup instructions, and obvious trust gaps.
howToBuild: Repo/site intake → build/test commands → link audit → screenshot pass → README check → launch-readiness scorecard → fix list.
monetization: Indie launch audit, agency QA service, or internal release gate.

name: Food Waste Archaeologist
description: Studies what you actually throw away or forget in the fridge, then changes your grocery list and meal plan. It treats food waste like a data problem, not a morality lecture.
howToBuild: Quick waste log → receipt/grocery import → meal plan notes → pattern detection → shopping list adjustment → weekly SMS with one change.
monetization: Household tool, sustainability workshop, or grocery planning product.

name: Neighborhood Noise Log
description: Keeps a timestamped record of recurring noise, nuisance, construction, traffic, or event issues. It turns scattered complaints into a clear timeline with evidence and calm language.
howToBuild: Mobile form → optional audio/photo attachment → event database → pattern summary → letter draft → export packet.
monetization: Personal admin tool, tenant support workflow, or civic data project.

name: “Explain My Benefits Letter” Reader
description: Reads a confusing benefits, insurance, school, tax, or government letter and extracts the actual decision, deadline, requested action, appeal path, and documents needed.
howToBuild: PDF/photo upload → OCR/text extraction → decision parser → deadline reminders → checklist page → appeal/request draft.
monetization: Personal admin helper, nonprofit intake tool, or caseworker support product.

name: Productized Service Foundry
description: Turns a messy skill into a productized service with a scope, price, intake form, delivery packet, payment link, and fulfillment checklist. Good for people who can do useful work but hate packaging it.
howToBuild: Skill/service intake → offer design rubric → Stripe product draft → landing page copy → intake form → delivery checklist → follow-up automation.
monetization: Direct use. This creates sellable services.

name: Personal Search Engine for Past Decisions
description: Lets you search your old decisions by situation: why you picked one tool, why a project was paused, why you said no, what evidence mattered, and whether the decision aged well.
howToBuild: Decision note template → workspace folder index → semantic/tag search → outcome review reminders → private zo.space search page.
monetization: Personal operating system feature, founder tool, or writing/research assistant.

name: Tiny Terms of Service Watcher
description: Watches terms, privacy policies, creator program rules, API limits, and pricing pages for changes that affect your work. It produces a plain-English “what changed and why it matters” note.
howToBuild: URL watchlist → scheduled fetch/diff → change classifier → impact summary → email alert → archive of previous versions.
monetization: Creator/business monitoring product or compliance-lite service.

name: The Polite Pestering Machine
description: Follows up on things that should not require heroic memory: refunds, invoices, applications, support tickets, medical records, contractor estimates, collaboration promises. It drafts calm follow-ups on schedule.
howToBuild: Follow-up item form → due date tracker → Gmail draft generator → SMS review reminder → status dashboard → escalation template.
monetization: Freelancer admin tool, personal assistant template, or small business workflow.

name: Pop-Up Research Desk
description: Creates a temporary research headquarters for one topic: source list, reading queue, claim tracker, interview questions, open tabs, citations, and a final brief. Useful when curiosity turns into a project and the browser starts breeding tabs.
howToBuild: Topic intake → web research → source database → citation notes → claim tracker → markdown research brief → optional public reading list page.
monetization: Research service, journalist workflow, creator research product, or paid briefing packet.

name: Decision Receipts for Teams
description: Creates a record of why a team made a decision before everyone rewrites history. It captures options, evidence, objections, owner, date, and what would make the team revisit it.
howToBuild: Decision form → meeting notes import → ADR-style markdown generator → searchable private page → revisit reminders → weekly digest.
monetization: Team ops template, consulting tool, or internal knowledge product.

name: Personal API Catalog
description: Keeps track of every API key, webhook, endpoint, integration, and service you rely on without storing secrets in the wrong place. It documents what exists and what would break if it changed.
howToBuild: Manual service registry → environment variable name references → endpoint checker → webhook test notes → private docs page → rotation reminders.
monetization: Dev ops template, freelancer handoff product, or internal reliability tool.

name: The “Is This Scammy?” Lens
description: Reviews an email, website, DM, invoice, job offer, product page, or fundraising request and explains the risk signals in plain language. It does not panic. It points to evidence.
howToBuild: Paste/upload intake → sender/domain checks → claim extraction → web corroboration → risk score → safe next steps.
monetization: Family tech support product, consumer safety tool, or public education site.

name: Hyperlocal FAQ Builder
description: Builds a public FAQ for questions people ask over and over in a town, organization, school, church, club, or neighborhood: hours, forms, rules, contacts, recurring events, and “who do I call?” answers.
howToBuild: Source document import → question extraction → answer drafting with citations → public zo.space FAQ → correction submission form → review queue.
monetization: Local web service, civic project, or maintenance retainer.

name: Personal Knowledge Decay Monitor
description: Finds notes, docs, and assumptions that are likely stale: old setup instructions, outdated prices, links that moved, plans that no longer match reality, and advice from a previous version of your life.
howToBuild: Workspace scan → last-modified analysis → link checking → stale-risk scoring → review queue → archive/update suggestions.
monetization: Knowledge-base cleanup service or personal ops tool.

name: Micro-SaaS Fulfillment Desk
description: Gives a tiny paid product the boring backend it needs: order intake, fulfillment status, customer messages, delivery files, refund notes, and weekly revenue summary.
howToBuild: Stripe orders → workspace customer folders → fulfillment checklist → Gmail draft templates → private dashboard → weekly sales email.
monetization: Direct use for paid templates, audits, reports, or small services.

name: Local Contractor Memory
description: Tracks contractor quotes, timelines, reliability, photos, warranties, and follow-up promises. Over time it becomes a private or shared memory of who actually showed up and what work cost.
howToBuild: Quote/photo upload → contact records → project timeline → cost table → review notes → warranty/reminder automation.
monetization: Personal household tool, neighborhood resource, or local homeowner service.

name: Open Source Maintainer Radar
description: Watches the projects you depend on for releases, breaking changes, maintainer burnout signals, security notices, and issue patterns before they surprise you.
howToBuild: GitHub repo watchlist → release/feed checks → issue/PR summary → dependency impact notes → weekly maintainer health brief.
monetization: Dev team monitoring service, consultant tool, or personal dependency dashboard.

name: The Humane Rejection Writer
description: Helps say no without sounding cold, fake, or over-explaining: invitations, collaborations, unpaid work, client scope creep, awkward requests, and opportunities that are not right.
howToBuild: Situation intake → relationship/context questions → boundary tone selector → draft generator → saved phrase bank.
monetization: Freelancer tool, coaching resource, or personal communication assistant.

name: Dataset Storyboarder
description: Turns a raw dataset into a set of story angles: what changed, what is weird, what is missing, which rows deserve inspection, and which chart would actually explain something.
howToBuild: CSV/DuckDB import → schema scan → anomaly checks → question generator → chart suggestions → markdown research memo.
monetization: Data journalism helper, analyst tool, or research service.

name: Private Job Search War Room
description: Runs a job search like a campaign: target companies, contact history, tailored resumes, application status, interview prep, follow-up drafts, and red-flag notes.
howToBuild: Job URL intake → company research → application tracker → resume/cover letter draft folder → calendar reminders → private dashboard.
monetization: Career coaching tool, personal use, or paid setup package.

name: The Small Town Web Reviver
description: Finds local organizations with broken, outdated, or missing web presence and turns that into a practical service pipeline: what is broken, who to contact, what a simple fix would cost, and a ready-to-send pitch.
howToBuild: Local organization source list → website scan → screenshot capture → issue scoring → offer page generator → outreach draft queue.
monetization: Freelance pipeline, local web service, or civic improvement project.
